questions.question: What does JSX stand for?
options: JavaScript XML
Extensible Stylesheet Language
JavaScript Extension
XHTML Syntax

questions.question: What is React?
options: A JavaScript framework for building user interfaces
A back-end server framework
A database management system
A styling library
questions.correctAnswer: A JavaScript framework for building user interfaces

questions.question: What function is used to change the state of a component in React?
options: this.setState()
this.modifyState()
this.updateState()
this.changeState()

questions.question: What is a prop in React?
options: A value that can be passed from a parent component to a child component
A built-in HTML attribute
A CSS property
A state variable
questions.correctAnswer: A value that can be passed from a parent component to a child component

questions.question: What lifecycle method is called after a component has been rendered?
options: componentWillMount
componentDidMount
componentDidRender
componentWillUnmount

questions.question: What is the purpose of React Router?
options: To enable navigation among views of various components in a React Application
To provide a way to write CSS in React components
To manage state in a React Application
To handle HTTP requests in a React Application
questions.correctAnswer: To enable navigation among views of various components in a React Application

questions.question: What is a React component?
options: A function or class that accepts input and returns a React element
A built-in HTML element in React
A predefined style in React
A JavaScript module in React
questions.correctAnswer: A function or class that accepts input and returns a React element

questions.question: What is the virtual DOM in React?
options: A lightweight copy of the actual DOM
A part of React used for virtual reality applications
A data structure used for React state management
A simulation of the browser's memory
questions.correctAnswer: A lightweight copy of the actual DOM

questions.question: What is the purpose of keys in React lists?
options: Keys are used to uniquely identify elements in a list and improve performance during rendering
Keys are used for encryption in React applications
Keys are used to define styles for elements in a React list
Keys are used to handle events in React lists
questions.correctAnswer: Keys are used to uniquely identify elements in a list and improve performance during rendering

questions.question: What is a higher-order component (HOC) in React?
options: A function that takes a component and returns a new component with additional functionality
A component that is placed higher in the component tree
A component that uses hooks extensively
A component that renders other components
questions.correctAnswer: A function that takes a component and returns a new component with additional functionality

questions.question: What is React Context?
options: A way to pass data through the component tree without having to pass props down manually
A component used for styling in React
A component used for handling form submissions in React
A component for managing global state in React
questions.correctAnswer: A way to pass data through the component tree without having to pass props down manually

questions.question: What is the purpose of the useEffect hook in React?
options: To perform side effects in your function components
To create animations in React components
To handle form submissions in React components
To manage global state in React components
questions.correctAnswer: To perform side effects in your function components
